def: n. f. S. भ्रू, P. abrū . The eyebrow. 1. Āṅkh jab tak hai to khush ātī hai bhauṅ, Āṅkh hī phūṭī to kab bhātī hai bhauṅ? Prov. The sparkling eye the arching brow its beauty lends, Its brightness quenched, all cold and dull it lifeless bends. 2. Aṅkhī torī lālī, bhaṅhuna tore chaṛhal kamānwā, re goriā! Bhoj. Song. Red lines shine in your eyes, your brows arched like a bow, fair maid! bhaweṅ tānnā, ṭeṛhī karnā, yā chaṛhānā , v. n. To knit or raise the eyebrows; frown.
more: n. f. The barking of a dog.

def: s.f. The eyebrow

(Answer to the question in the Ex. under augan (1)). def: s.m. Breaking, separation, disunion, difference, disagreement, interruption, disturbance; betrayal; breach, rupture, fracture; fissure, chasm, cleft; separation, difference, distinction, peculiarity; discrimination, discernment; kind, sort, species, variety; device; secrecy, secret, mystery; secret or hidden virtues or resources (of)
more: n. m. S. भिद् to break. 1. Difference; variance; dissimilarity; discrepancy. 2. Secrecy; secret; mystery; secret intention or purpose. Is meṅ bhī kuchh bhed hai . Prov. There is some mystery here. 3. Problem; puzzle; riddle; enigma. 4. Knowledge; cognizance; ken. Ghar kā bhed jab hī pāyā, jab chauk puran ko ḍhaknā āyā . Prov. The house resources only then found out, When for the ' chauk puran ' the cup's brought out. (An earthen cup, instead of a brass or silver one, shows the poverty of the house). bhed pānā , v. n. 1. To find out a secret; get a clue. 2. To solve (a problem; riddle, enigma). bhed denā , v. n. To give a clue; to utter or betray a secret. bhed rakh'nā , v. n. To keep secret; keep one's own counsel. bhed kholnā , v. n. To divulge; expose; betray; unmask. bhed lenā , v. n. To spy; pry into; reconnoitre; find a clue.
s. m. 1. A secret, secrecy, mystery. 2. Separation, difference. بهيدلينا bhed-lenā, v. a. To spy, to pry into, to work oneʼs self into confidence, to sound.
subs: v.n. To find out a secret, discover the secret (of), solve (a problem, riddle, &c.)
(-ko), To give a clue (to); to divulge or betray a secret
(-kā), To keep a secret; to keep one's own counsel
(-meṅ; -kā), To distinguish, discriminate; to classify, sort
(-kā), To divulge a secret, to betray; expose, unmask
(kā), To spy, pry into, get at the secret (of), worm ooneself into another's confidence, to sound.

wety: Perhaps from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀲𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀤𑀺 (visajjadi), from Sanskrit विसर्जयति (visarjayati, “to send, utter”). Turner disagrees, and reconstructs Ashokan Prakrit *𑀪𑁂𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀢𑀺 (*bhejjati), possibly from a Sanskrit अभि-अज्यते (abhi-ajyate, “to be led to”). def: n. f. S. भिक्ष् to beg for. Alms; charity. 1. Bin māṅge motī mileṅ, māṅgī mile na bhīk . Prov. Pearls for those who ask not; who ask, not alms e'en in their lot. 2. more: n. m. S. भी to fear. 1. An epithet of Shiv, but more especially an inferior manifestation of that deity. Of these eight are reckoned, all of which allude to terrific properties of mind or body. 2. A rāg or musical mode calculated to excite emotions of terror. It is sung at early dawn in autumn, and is represented in pictures by Mahadeo with the Ganges flowing from the hair of his head, and this musical deity ( rāg ) issuing from his mouth. The scheme commonly used to this rāg is as follows —6+4+4+2, +6+4+2=28 instants, with pause at the 16th instant. The metre is complete in two lines. Kellogg's Gram. bhai'roṅ , adj. Fearful; horrible. Mahā bhairoṅ rūp hai .

def: adv. S. अभ्यन्तर Inside; in within; ( andar ); in-doors; within doors. bhītrā bāhirā . adv. Half out half in. bhītar ānā , v. n. To come in; enter; gain admittance; effect an entrance. bhītar honā , v. n. To be contained, included, comprehended.
adv. (dakh.) Within: see بهيتر bhītar.
adv. (dakh.) Within: see بهيتر bhītar.
adj. Interior, internal, inward, inner, inside; hidden, secret
more: adj. 1. Inner; internal; inmost; interior; inward. 2. Hidden; concealed; secret. Bhītar kā ghāo, Rānī jāne ki Rāo . Prov. Domestic sores the wife and husband only know. bhītrī chāl , n. f. ( Physics ). Endosmose. bhītrī kon . ( Math .) Interior angle ( zāviyah-i-andarūnī ). bhītrī yā guptī mār , n. f. Internal hurt or injury.
adj. Inward, inside, internal, inner, inferiour.

def: s.m.f. The people who live in a house or family (opp. to bāhariyā, 'outsiders'), inmates; domestic; one admitted to the inner apartments of a house; guest at a marriage feast (who eats in company with the relations of the bride); — one who betrays a secret.
more: adj. used substantively, 1. The people who live in a house, not strangers, domestick. 2. Men who preside at a temple (i. e. بهنڐاري A steward; پوجاري A priest; رسوٴيا rasoʼiyā, A cook). 3. Those among the guests at a wedding feast who eat in company with the relations of the bride (those, who partake of the feast without, being called باهريا).

def: E. n. Opp. of bāhariyā , 1. A domestic, male or female; one admitted to the inner apartments of a dwelling. 2. A guest at a marriage feast who is admitted to eat with the relatives of the bride. 3. One who betrays secrets.
s.m. Ground rent paid for the site of a house (by a stranger).
s.f. Fear, apprehension, alarm; trembling.
s.f. = bhīt, q.v.
s.f. Wall; breadth of a wall; embankment; vestige of an old house.
more: n. f. 1. Touch; contact; collision. 2. The act of meeting; an interview; a visit; introduction. Dukhte choṭ, kanauṅḍe bheṭ . Prov. The part that's sore is afflicted more. (Misfortunes never come single). 3. Sacrifice; offerings to superiors ( bheṭ-pūjā ); a complimentary gift ( nazrānā ). 4. Hymns in praise of some goddess. Hin. Pahle mātā kī bheṭeṅ gā lo . Wom. 5. A bribe; douceur; an illegal gratification. bheṭ chaṛhānā, denā, yā karnā , v. a. To offer; present; sacrifice. bheṭ honā , v. n. 1. To meet; encounter; have an interview. 2. To be sacrificed; to fall a victim. Rel kī bheṭ ho gayā. bheṭ bakrā , n. m. 1. The sacrifice of a goat. 2. The complimentary presentation of a goat to a superior.

1. (for भित्ति ) s. f. A wall, or breadth of a wall. 2. (for भीत ) adj. Fearful. اوچهي کي پريت جون بالوکي بهيت ochhe kī prīt joṅ bālū kī bhīt, The friendship of the mean is like a wall of sand (i. e. unstable).

ety: S वृत्, or अभ्यावृत्; see bheṭ
def: v.n. To come in contact (with, -se), be touched (by); to be defiled or polluted by contact (with).
s.f. Nipple (of a woman's breast).
s. f. (dakh.) Nipple ( Harris ): see بهٿني bhiṭnī.
s.f. Wall ( = bhīt, q.v.); breaking; a thing broken or divided; fragment; figure; defect; place, spot; opportunity, occasion; asylum.
more: n. m. A learned man; scholar; philosopher; a title of a Mahārāshṭrī Brāhman.
n. f. 1. ( bhaṭṭī ). A fire place; furnace; kiln. Bhaṭ paṛe woh sonā jis se ṭūṭe kān ! Prov. Fire burn the gold whose earrings split the ears! 2. (S. भिद to break). A hole; pit; cave; den.

def: s.f. Wall ( = bhīt, q.v.); small house lizard.
s.f. Fear, apprehension, dread ( = bhay).
wety: PIE word *bʰréh₂tēr A diminutive of بھائی (bhā'ī, “brother”), from Sanskrit भ्रातृ (bhrātṛ), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *bʰréh₂tēr (“brother”).

def: s.f. Coral.
s.m. = bhoṛal, q.v.
n. m. Mica ( abrak ).

s.m. Enjoyment, fruition; eating; use, application; usufruct, use of a deposit; utility, advantage, profitable aim or object; possessing, possession; cherishing, nourishing, protecting; suffering, experiencing; enduring, feeling, perception (of joy or sorrow), pleasure, satisfaction, gratification; suffering, passion; requital; abuse, rudeness, insult; any object of enjoyment, that which is eaten, food, victuals, repast; sexual enjoyment, carnal intercourse; dressed food offered to an idol; morning hymns, matins; — gain, profit, produce; money, wealth; hire, hire of dancing-girls or courtezans; (in Arith.) numerator of a fraction
more: n. m. S. भुज् to eat, enjoy. 1. Food offered to an idol. 2. Morning hymns; matins. 3. Pleasure; enjoyment; zest. 4. Use; enjoyment; ( in law ) usufruct. 5. Sexual intercourse ( kām ); animal gratification. Gyān baṛhe soch se, rog baṛhe bhog se . Prov. Knowledge with thought increaseth more, from pleasure comes diseases sore. 6. Abusive words; abuse; insult. 7. ( Arith .) The numerator of a fraction ( ans ). bhog-bilās , n. m. Sensual pleasure; sexual intercourse. bhog sunānā, yā denā , Wom. v. n. To abuse; call by foul names. Kisī pyāron-pīṭī ne merā nām līyā, to hazāroṅ bhog sunāūṅgī . Wom. If any mourner-cursed dares take my name, Foul epithets a thousand I'll proclaim. bhog karnā , v. n. To have or enjoy sexual intercourse. bhog lagānā , v. a. 1. To eat; take food; dine. Tum bhī bhog lagā lo . 2. To offer food or dainties to an idol. Srī Krishn, mahārāj, ko bhog lagā do .
s. m. 1. Enjoyment, suffering. 2. Pleasure, satisfaction. 3. Possession. 4. Eating, victuals. بهوگ کرنا bhog karnā, To enjoy, to suffer.
subs: (bhoga+adhikāra), s.m. Possession of the usufruct of mortgaged or pledged property; — bhog-ādhikārī, s.m. Possessor of the usufruct of mortgaged or pledged property
(bhoga+vilāsa), s.m. Luxurious living; sensual pleasure, amorous enjoyment, sexual intercourse; — bhog-bilāsī, s.m. One who is addicted to luxuriousness, or to sensual pleasure, voluptuary
s.m. Mortgage with possession of the property by the mortgagee and of the usufruct thereof; bond granting the use of a pledged article
s.m. Person in actual possession of anything; possessor; lord, governor
s.m. Possessor or enjoyer of any property
bhog sunānā, v.t. To abuse, vilify
adj. & s.m. Producing or affording enjoyment; procuring food or pleasure; — one who affords enjoyment
v.t. To experience, suffer, undergo; to enjoy; to be requited for; to have or enjoy sexual intercourse with
bhog lagānā or lagā-denā or laga-lenā
v.t. To eat, partake of food; to place food before (one); to offer food to an idol
(fem. of next), s.f. A woman who yields enjoyment
adj. & s.m. Furnished with enjoyment, yielding enjoyment, delightful, pleasurable, enjoyable; — one who yields enjoyment.

def: s.m. Snake; expanded hood of a snake; the body; particular array of troops, army in column
subs: (fem. of next), s.f. Name of the capital of the serpent race; name of the Ganges of the lower regions
adj. & s.m. Furnished with windings or curves; — a snake.
